reminder.notes:
Two phones stay in step: the right alarm on the right phone

Follow-up to the cross-device work, from a full feature-by-feature audit. Every
action on one phone now keeps your other phone correctly in sync.

### Features
- Complete or Dismiss on one phone now updates your other phone right away, the
  same way Snooze already did -- the other phone re-arms or clears its own alarm
  within seconds instead of at its next open.
- Your preferences now sync across phones: reminder tone, ring duration, snooze
  duration, snooze cycles, after-final-snooze action, time format, and default
  home view. Change them on one phone and the other follows.
- A snoozed reminder now shows a "Snoozed - rings 3:45 pm" badge, and its snooze
  time as the next ring. It appears on both phones, so you can see that a snooze
  taken on one phone reached the other.

### Bug fixes
- Fixed: a reminder you delete, pause, or complete on one phone could still ring
  on your other phone. Removing or finishing it now also cancels the alarm that
  was armed on the other phone.
- Fixed: after an action taken elsewhere, a phone could keep a stale alarm armed
  and fire it at the old time. The sync now cancels alarms that no longer apply,
  not just add new ones.

cards.notes:
Cards 2.5.0 — Background update checks, and no more double-downloading updates

### Features
- Cards now checks for updates periodically in the background (about every 6 hours) and posts a notification when one is found, in addition to the existing manual "Check for Updates" in Settings. Tapping the notification opens the app; the download and install still happen from the Settings sheet.

### Bug fixes
- The update downloader now persists its in-flight DownloadManager id and re-attaches to a still-running download instead of deleting the partial file and starting over — fixes re-downloading the same update from zero after backgrounding or reopening the app mid-update.
- The update sheet now re-syncs its progress on resume instead of occasionally showing a frozen progress bar.

ai-scanner.notes:
AI Scanner 1.4.1 — Background update checks, and no more double-downloading updates

### Features
- AI Scanner now checks for updates periodically in the background (about every 6 hours) and posts a notification when one is found, in addition to the existing manual "Check for Updates" in Settings. Tapping the notification opens the app; the download and install still happen from the Settings sheet.

### Bug fixes
- The update downloader now persists its in-flight DownloadManager id and re-attaches to a still-running download instead of deleting the partial file and starting over — fixes re-downloading the same update from zero after backgrounding or reopening the app mid-update.
- The update sheet now re-syncs its progress on resume instead of occasionally showing a frozen progress bar.

mirrordrive.notes:
Show the app version and PNSJY branding in Settings

Settings now ends with the current app version and the PNSJY store branding, so every
PNSJY app presents the same footer.

### Features

- The Settings screen now shows the installed app version and a PNSJY brand footer as its
  last two items, consistent across all PNSJY apps.

### Bug fixes

No user-facing bug fixes in this release.

localsender.notes:
Local Sender 1.0.11 - security hardening (full audit)

A full security audit (see AUDIT.md and THREAT_MODEL.md in the repo) and fixes for
every CRITICAL and HIGH finding it turned up. All local content is now encrypted
at rest, the macOS build is sandboxed again, and Drive backup no longer stores its
own key next to the data it protects.

### Features
- Google Drive backup is now sealed with a passphrase you set and never upload —
  Drive alone can no longer read a backup, only you can.
- A warning dialog when a device presents a different identity key than the one
  already trusted under that name, instead of an ordinary pairing prompt.

### Bug fixes
- Chat history, rooms, trusted-peer keys, peer profiles, network profiles, and
  device name are now encrypted at rest (previously plaintext in local storage).
- Fixed a path-traversal bug where an incoming file offer's name could write
  outside the downloads folder.
- Fixed an unbounded network frame length that let any device on the LAN push
  memory usage toward the app's limit with a single unauthenticated connection.
- A silent, unresponsive peer during pairing could lock the app out of pairing
  with anyone else; pairing now times out and recovers.
- A connection dropped mid-file-transfer no longer leaves a frozen progress row
  or an orphaned partial file on disk.
- Re-enabled the macOS App Sandbox (previously off since v1.0.1 to work around a
  Keychain hang); the underlying hang is now handled by an existing timeout and
  fallback, so sandboxing no longer breaks pairing.
- The identity key's on-disk fallback file (used only when the OS secure store is
  unavailable) is now encrypted instead of holding the raw key.
